rationale: Marketing Manager with career spanning Wipro (IT services) and Initech (fictional company), with only peripheral web skills (React, Flask) and zero ML/IR/ranking experience; AI curiosity limited to ChatGPT productivity experiments.

Accountant/Content Writer/Civil Engineer whose entire career is at fictional companies (Pied Piper, Hooli) and non-technical roles with no engineering or ML output; skills list (Kafka, Marketing, SQL, Redux) shows no IR/ranking relevance.

Conventional backend/cloud engineer with product-company stints at Swiggy and CRED, but ML exposure is self-learner only (small RAG side project), top skills are Airflow beginner and Node.js beginner, and there is no production embeddings, vector-search, or ranking experience.

Entire 5.8-year career at IT-services giants Wipro and TCS in Accountant and Operations Manager roles with no ML or engineering work; skills (AWS beginner 5 months, ETL, Speech Recognition) show no IR/ranking depth.

Cloud/mobile engineer whose career includes Dunder Mifflin (fictional company), Wipro, and Cognizant (IT services), with top skill being GANs (CV-focused, not NLP/IR) and no production search, embeddings, or ranking system experience.

CMU B.E. Data Science (tier_1 education) and a Flipkart product-company stint are positive signals, but only 3.5 years total experience, role was cloud/DevOps not ML, and AI knowledge is self-learner level with no production retrieval or ranking work.

6.5 years predominantly at IT-services firms (Capgemini twice, Accenture) in mobile/frontend roles, with a brief Razorpay stint; skills are Salesforce CRM, React beginner, and REST APIs — no ML, embeddings, or ranking experience evident.

IIT Roorkee M.S. Machine Learning is a strong education signal, but the only career role is Operations Manager at Hooli (fictional company) doing brand design and creative direction — no production ML or engineering work at all.

Entire career at two fictional companies (Stark Industries, Initech), primary expertise is TTS and Speech Recognition which the JD explicitly excludes, and skills are heavily inflated (advanced Data Science/TTS for a 4.3-year career).

5.5 years of production ML at product companies (Yellow.ai, Ola, Freshworks) with Learning to Rank listed as advanced (34 months), Pinecone advanced (31 months), XGBoost usage, and recommendation-system work at Ola; held back by a Computer Vision/YOLO focus as top skill, junior title despite tenure, and no explicit embeddings-based hybrid retrieval or NDCG evaluation ownership.
